title: rudbeckia fulgida var. palustris
intro: Rudbeckia fulgida var. palustris, also known as the green-headed coneflower, is a perennial plant species belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the leaves, flowers, and roots of Rudbeckia fulgida var. palustris are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is necessary to confirm its efficacy and safety.
